ddls:
CREATE TABLE "virtual_machines" (
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each virtual machine */,
  "vm_name" TEXT /* Name of the virtual machine */,
  "datacenter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the datacenter where the VM is located */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cluster where the VM is managed */,
  "host_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the host system on which the VM runs */,
  "uuid" TEXT /* Unique identifier (UUID) for the VM */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the VM (e.g., running, stopped) */,
  "os_type" TEXT /* Type of operating system running on the VM */,
  "cpu_count" INTEGER /* Number of virtual CPUs allocated to the VM */,
  "memory_size" REAL /* Amount of memory allocated to the VM (in GB) */,
  "storage_size" REAL /* Total storage size allocated to the VM (in GB) */,
  "network_interfaces" TEXT /* List of network interfaces configured for the VM */,
  "power_state" TEXT /* Current power state of the VM (e.g., on, off) */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the VM was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the VM was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_virtual_machines_host_id FOREIGN KEY ("host_id") REFERENCES hosts ("host_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_virtual_machines_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_virtual_machines_datacenter_id FOREIGN KEY ("datacenter_id") REFERENCES datacenters ("datacenter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datacenters" (
  "datacenter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each datacenter */,
  "dc_name" TEXT /* Name of the datacenter */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the datacenter */,
  "capacity" REAL /* Total capacity of the datacenter (in GB) */,
  "availability_zone" TEXT /* Availability zone of the datacenter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("datacenter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "cluster_name" TEXT /* Name of the cluster */,
  "datacenter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the datacenter where the cluster is located */,
  "resource_pools" TEXT /* Names of resource pools associated with the cluster */,
  "high_availability" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if high availability is enabled */,
  "distributed_resource_scheduling" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if distributed resource scheduling is enabled */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hosts" (
  "host_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each host */,
  "host_name" TEXT /* Name of the host system */,
  "datacenter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the datacenter where the host is located */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cluster where the host is managed */,
  "cpu_count" INTEGER /* Number of physical CPUs on the host */,
  "memory_size" REAL /* Amount of memory on the host (in GB) */,
  "storage_size" REAL /* Total storage size on the host (in GB) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the host (e.g., up, down) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the host system */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the host system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("host_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine for which metrics are recorded */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metrics were collected */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage in GB */,
  "disk_io" REAL /* Disk I/O operations per second */,
  "network_io" REAL /* Network I/O operations per second */,
  "cpu_ready_time" REAL /* CPU ready time in milliseconds */,
  "cpu_wait_time" REAL /* CPU wait time in milliseconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine involved in the incident */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the incident */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the incident (e.g., open, resolved) */,
  "reported_date" TEXT /* Date when the incident was reported */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date when the incident was resolved */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the incident (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the incident (e.g., performance, security) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, operator, viewer) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine associated with the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., performance, configuration) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the alert (e.g., new, acknowledged) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "object_type" TEXT /* Type of object affected by the action */,
  "object_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier of the object affected by the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_status" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the service (e.g., running, stopped) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last status check */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was sent */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the notification has been read */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., alert, incident) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role assigned to the user */,
  "assigned_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "backup_jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each backup job */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine being backed up */,
  "backup_type" TEXT /* Type of backup (e.g., full, incremental) */,
  "schedule" TEXT /* Schedule for the backup job */,
  "last_run" TEXT /* Date of the last backup job run */,
  "next_run" TEXT /* Date of the next backup job run */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_backup_jobs_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "replication_jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each replication job */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine being replicated */,
  "replication_type" TEXT /* Type of replication (e.g., synchronous, asynchronous) */,
  "schedule" TEXT /* Schedule for the replication job */,
  "last_run" TEXT /* Date of the last replication job run */,
  "next_run" TEXT /* Date of the next replication job run */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replication_jobs_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)
